Can you check what is actually happening with it and tell me what I should do next?

content.content:
content: "# Web Document

## Metadata

- title: Where is my package? Tracking\
  \ Status Help - FAQ | USPS
- url: https://faq.usps.com/articles/FAQ/Where-is-my-package
\
  - source: United States Postal Service
- domain: faq.usps.com

## Content

\
  WHERE IS MY PACKAGE? TRACKING STATUS HELP

USPS uses different tracking statuses\
  \ for different lifecycle stages. The correct next step depends on the exact current\
  \ status and on how long that status has remained unresolved.

### Delivery Status\
  \ Not Updated / Awaiting Delivery Scan

This status means a Delivered scan was\
  \ not recorded by 9 p.m. local time on the date the package was Out for Delivery.\
  \ The normal expectation is delivery on the next business day.

Do not treat that\
  \ normal expectation as an unlimited waiting rule. If more than one full postal\
  \ business day has passed since this status and no delivery has occurred, use the\
  \ tracking number to submit a service request to the local Post Office.

### Out\
  \ for Delivery

The package has been prepared for delivery at the local Post Office\
  \ and is expected to be delivered that day. If it has not been delivered after one\
  \ full postal business day following the scan, a service request can be submitted\
  \ to the local Post Office using the tracking number.

### Arrived at Post Office
\
  
The package has reached the local Post Office and is being prepared for delivery.\
  \ It should normally arrive that day or the next postal business day.

If there\
  \ has been no tracking change after one full postal business day following the arrival\
  \ date and no delivery has occurred, use the tracking number to submit a service\
  \ request to the local Post Office.

### Notice Left — no authorized recipient\
  \ available

This status means a signature was required and nobody was available\
  \ to sign. A notice is left and there is no automatic second delivery attempt. Depending\
  \ on the item, the customer can schedule redelivery, pick up the item, or request\
  \ return to sender.

### Available for Pickup

This status means the package\
  \ cannot be delivered and is being held at the local Post Office for collection.\
  \ Redelivery cannot be scheduled for a package in this status.

### USPS in Possession\
  \ of Item

USPS has received the package and will process it toward the delivering\
  \ Post Office. No special action is normally needed while it continues through the\
  \ network.

### Redelivery statuses

A Redelivery Scheduled status or Reminder\
  \ to Schedule Redelivery status applies to a delivery-recovery workflow. These statuses\
  \ are different from Delivery Status Not Updated / Awaiting Delivery Scan and should\
  \ not be treated as interchangeable.
"
metadata: {}
title: Where is my package? Tracking Status Help - FAQ | USPS
url: https://faq.usps.com/articles/FAQ/Where-is-my-package

content: '# Web Document


  ## Metadata


  - title: Help with problems in shipment tracking | DHL Private Customer Service

  - url: https://www.dhl.de/en/privatkunden/hilfe-kundenservice/sendungsverfolgung/probleme-loesungen.html

  - source: DHL Paket GmbH

  - domain: dhl.de


  ## Content


  HELP WITH PROBLEMS IN SHIPMENT TRACKING


  ### Shipment not recognized in tracking


  If a tracking number is not recognized, first check that the number was entered
  correctly. A new shipment can take some time to appear in the tracking system. Different
  products may also use different tracking services, and some shipment types do not
  include tracking.


  When the shipment was sent through another parcel provider, the relevant carrier''s
  tracking service should be checked instead.


  ### Estimated delivery time not met


  An estimated delivery date is a forecast rather than a guaranteed time. Delays can
  occur because of weather, traffic, sorting problems, manual reworking, or damaged
  packaging that must be secured before onward transport.


  Customer service generally cannot contact a delivery agent directly or accelerate
  a parcel while it is moving through the network.


  ### Shipment taking unusually long or status not changing


  For an ordinary DHL shipment, a short delay can occur. When the status has not changed
  for fewer than five business days, the guidance is to continue monitoring the current
  tracking information.


  If the shipment status has not changed for more than five business days, an investigation
  can be requested. Recipients are advised to consult the sender first because the
  sender can often resolve an order-related delivery issue more quickly.


  This investigation guidance is based on an unchanged tracking record. Before applying
  it to a specific shipment, the current status and the carrier responsible for the
  shipment should be identified.


  ### Tracking numbers


  A tracking number is a combination of letters and numbers used to identify a shipment
  in the tracking system. It is different from a retailer''s order number.


  Senders can usually find the tracking number on a posting receipt or shipping confirmation.
  Recipients normally receive the tracking number from the sender or online shop.


  Tracking requires a valid shipment number. When the current shipment is being handled
  outside the tracking system being checked, another carrier''s tracking service may
  be needed to obtain later events.

  '
metadata: {}
title: Help with problems in shipment tracking | DHL Private Customer Service
url: https://www.dhl.de/en/privatkunden/hilfe-kundenservice/sendungsverfolgung/probleme-loesungen.html

Your package is **not actually stuck in Germany**.

### What the tracking shows
- **Shipment:** DHL Paket from Germany to the United States  
- **DHL’s last origin scan:** Rodgau, Germany — “transported to the destination country/area” on **June 18**
- **International handoff:** Completed from DHL to **USPS** on **June 25**
- **USPS tracking number:** `9405509205568123456789`
- **Latest USPS event:** **“Delivery Status Not Updated / Awaiting Delivery Scan”** in **Los Angeles, CA 90017**, recorded **June 30**
- As of the check on **July 2**, there was no delivery scan, delivery attempt, notice-left event, or return-to-sender record.

### What that means
The package has reached the USPS delivery network and appears to have been at or near the Los Angeles delivery stage. USPS says this status normally means a delivery scan was not recorded and delivery is generally expected the next business day. Since **two postal business days** have elapsed, it is reasonable to escalate rather than simply keep waiting.

### What to do next
1. **Check the USPS tracking page using the USPS number above**, not only the DHL number.
2. **Submit a USPS service request** for the local Post Office serving ZIP code **90017**, using that tracking number.
3. **Contact the sender or retailer today.** DHL advises recipients to consult the sender when an international shipment’s status has not changed; the sender can often initiate an investigation or resolve an order issue more effectively.
4. If USPS cannot locate it after the service request, ask the sender to open a **DHL investigation/claim**, subject to the shipment’s insurance and service terms.

There is currently **no indication of a failed delivery, missed notice, or return to sender**. DHL’s general guidance is to consider an investigation after more than five business days with no status change, but because USPS is now the responsible destination operator and its delivery-status scan is already overdue, the USPS service request and sender escalation are the appropriate next steps.

final_answer:
Your package is **not actually stuck in Germany**.

### What the tracking shows
- **Shipment:** DHL Paket from Germany to the United States  
- **DHL’s last origin scan:** Rodgau, Germany — “transported to the destination country/area” on **June 18**
- **International handoff:** Completed from DHL to **USPS** on **June 25**
- **USPS tracking number:** `9405509205568123456789`
- **Latest USPS event:** **“Delivery Status Not Updated / Awaiting Delivery Scan”** in **Los Angeles, CA 90017**, recorded **June 30**
- As of the check on **July 2**, there was no delivery scan, delivery attempt, notice-left event, or return-to-sender record.

### What that means
The package has reached the USPS delivery network and appears to have been at or near the Los Angeles delivery stage. USPS says this status normally means a delivery scan was not recorded and delivery is generally expected the next business day. Since **two postal business days** have elapsed, it is reasonable to escalate rather than simply keep waiting.

### What to do next
1. **Check the USPS tracking page using the USPS number above**, not only the DHL number.
2. **Submit a USPS service request** for the local Post Office serving ZIP code **90017**, using that tracking number.
3. **Contact the sender or retailer today.** DHL advises recipients to consult the sender when an international shipment’s status has not changed; the sender can often initiate an investigation or resolve an order issue more effectively.
4. If USPS cannot locate it after the service request, ask the sender to open a **DHL investigation/claim**, subject to the shipment’s insurance and service terms.

There is currently **no indication of a failed delivery, missed notice, or return to sender**. DHL’s general guidance is to consider an investigation after more than five business days with no status change, but because USPS is now the responsible destination operator and its delivery-status scan is already overdue, the USPS service request and sender escalation are the appropriate next steps.
